_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: MRT3 Circle Line Capped at RM45 Billion, Down from RM68 Billion in 2018
context: MRT3 received final approval in 2025 with a budget cap of approximately RM45 billion: about RM31 billion for construction and RM6-7 billion for land acquisition. The 2018 estimate had been RM68 billion before cancellation and revival.

cards.text: MRT3 Circle Line capped at RM45 billion, reduced from the RM68 billion 2018 estimate before cancellation and revival.
cards.sub: Construction allocation about RM31 billion plus RM6-7 billion for land acquisition. Final approval received in 2025.

cards.h: The project was cancelled in 2018 over cost concerns and revived in 2023. Every revival round adds review layers without binding caps.
cards.s: Land acquisition is targeted for completion in 2026; construction expected from 2027.
cards.d: The project was cancelled in 2018 over cost concerns and revived in 2023. Every revival round adds review layers without binding caps. Land acquisition is targeted for completion in 2026; construction expected from 2027.

cards.h: 51.6km elevated circle with 10 interchange stations linking existing MRT, LRT, KTM and Monorail lines.
cards.s: Designed to close the Klang Valley rail loop. Operational target: 2032.
cards.d: 51.6km elevated circle with 10 interchange stations linking existing MRT, LRT, KTM and Monorail lines. Designed to close the Klang Valley rail loop. Operational target: 2032.

cards.h: Even at the reduced cap, MRT3 remains Malaysia's largest single rail project. Fiscal scrutiny continues at every revision.
cards.s: Independent audit of engineering and procurement cost assumptions has been called for at multiple cost-revision rounds.
cards.d: Even at the reduced cap, MRT3 remains Malaysia's largest single rail project. Fiscal scrutiny continues at every revision. Independent audit of engineering and procurement cost assumptions has been called for at multiple cost-revision rounds.

cards.text: The story is not 'budget ballooning'. It is whether even the reduced cap holds through construction.
Reducing the headline figure does not eliminate cost-overrun risk. The discipline test is whether 2027-2032 construction stays within RM45 billion.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
